message.content:
Convert `/home/garrosh/repos/warcraft-components/src/App.tsx` from SolidJS to React. This is a 4360-line file. Read it fully in chunks (read 500 lines at a time across all 4360 lines), then write the complete React version.

## Context

The repo is a Warcraft III UI component library showcase. It's being converted from SolidJS to React.

**Already done:**
- `vite.config.ts` now uses `@vitejs/plugin-react` and aliases `@wc3-ui/react` → `packages/react/src/index.ts`
- `tsconfig.json` now has `"jsx": "react-jsx"`, `"jsxImportSource": "react"`
- `src/index.tsx` now uses `createRoot` from react-dom/client
- `src/state/race.ts` now just re-exports `RACE_PREFIXES`, `RACES` from `@wc3-ui/core` — the `currentRace` / `setCurrentRace` signals are GONE. Race state must live as `useState` in App.tsx.
- `packages/react/src/` contains all ported React components: `WC3RendererProvider`, `useWC3Renderer`, `Tooltip`, `EditBox`, `MenuPanel`, `StatBar`, `RadioButton`, `LoadingBar`, `Slider`, `CursorOverlay`, `BottomHud`, `TopHud`, `TopHudWebGL`, `HeroPortraitModel`, `TimeIndicatorModel`, `WorkerUnitModel`

## Key packages available
- `@wc3-ui/react` — import all the above components from here
- `@wc3-ui/core` — import `Race`, `RACE_PREFIXES`, `RACES` from here
- `war3-model` — `decodeBLP`, `getBLPImageData`, `parseMDX`
- `gl-matrix` — already installed

## SolidJS → React conversion rules

Apply these transformations consistently throughout:

| SolidJS | React |
|---------|-------|
| `import { createSignal, createEffect, onMount, onCleanup, For } from 'solid-js'` | `import { useState, useEffect, useRef, useCallback } from 'react'` |
| `const [x, setX] = createSignal(v)` | `const [x, setX] = useState(v)` |
| `x()` (reading a signal) | `x` (just the value) |
| `setX(v)` | `setX(v)` (same) |
| `setX(prev => ...)` | `setX(prev => ...)` (same) |
| `createEffect(() => { dep(); ... })` | `useEffect(() => { ... }, [dep])` — identify deps from what's read |
| `onMount(() => { ...; onCleanup(() => ...) })` | `useEffect(() => { ...; return () => ... }, [])` |
| `onCleanup(() => ...)` at component level | return cleanup from useEffect |
| `let ref!: HTMLEl` | `const ref = useRef<HTMLEl>(null)` |
| `ref` (direct use) | `ref.current` |
| `ref={el}` (JSX binding) | `ref={ref}` (pass the ref object) |
| `class="x"` | `className="x"` |
| `classList={{ x: cond() }}` | `className={\`base\${cond ? ' x' : ''}\`}` or template literal combining classes |
| `style={{ 'background-color': 'red' }}` | `style={{ backgroundColor: 'red' }}` — camelCase all CSS properties |
| `<For each={arr}>{(item) => <X />}</For>` | `{arr.map((item) => <X key={item} />)}` |
| `<Show when={cond()}><X /></Show>` | `{cond && <X />}` |
| `<Switch><Match when={a()}><A /></Match><Match when={b()}><B /></Match></Switch>` | `{a ? <A /> : b ? <B /> : null}` |
| `import type { Accessor } from 'solid-js'` | Remove — not needed |
| `props.race` (SolidJS props) | `{ race }` (destructured) |
| `() => value` (accessor as prop) | `value` (plain value) |

## State management

The main App() function uses many SolidJS signals. Convert them all to `useState`:
- `currentRace` → `const [race, setRace] = useState<Race>('Human')`
- Resource counters, bar percentages, UI state → all `useState`
- Non-reactive mutable values → `useRef`

## Renderer

The original App uses a `Renderer` from `@wc3-ui/core` created manually. In the React version:
- Wrap the entire App JSX in `<WC3RendererProvider race={race}>` from `@wc3-ui/react`
- Remove manual renderer creation, canvas setup, `renderer.init()`, `renderer.start()`, etc.
- Pass `race` prop to `WC3RendererProvider` so it loads the correct textures

## Components to import from `@wc3-ui/react`

These are already ported — use them directly instead of the SolidJS originals:
- `WC3RendererProvider` — wraps the whole app
- `TopHud` — takes `race`, `gold`, `lumber`, `onRaceChange` props
- `BottomHud` — takes `race` prop
- `TopHudWebGL` — takes `race`, `leftOnly?` props
- `StatBar` — takes `label`, `type`, `fillPercent`, `maxValue?`, `height?`, `hasBorder?`
- `Tooltip` — takes `children`
- `EditBox` — takes `text?`
- `MenuPanel` — no props
- `RadioButton` — takes `label`, `selected: boolean`, `onSelect`
- `LoadingBar` — takes `progress: number`
- `Slider` — takes `label`, `initialValue?`
- `CursorOverlay` — takes `race`
- `HeroPortraitModel` — takes `race`
- `TimeIndicatorModel` — takes `race`
- `WorkerUnitModel` — takes `race`, `side: 'left' | 'right'`

## Important details

1. The file has many inline canvas-drawing utility functions at the top (lines ~282-960). These are pure TypeScript with no SolidJS — keep them exactly as-is.
2. Inline component definitions within App.tsx (like `MenuFrameVariantCard`, etc.) need the SolidJS → React conversion.
3. For `createEffect` that runs async work and cancels on cleanup, use this pattern:
   ```tsx
   useEffect(() => {
     let cancelled = false;
     void doAsync().then(result => { if (!cancelled) setState(result); });
     return () => { cancelled = true; };
   }, [dep1, dep2]);
   ```
4. For canvas refs in inline components, use `useRef<HTMLCanvasElement>(null)` and access via `ref.current`.
5. The top-level App() has `setInterval` / RAF loops — put these in `useEffect(() => {...}, [])`.
6. All `renderer.xxx.push(...)` calls in App are replaced by using the correct `@wc3-ui/react` component (which handles registration internally).
7. Section components imported from `./components/sections/` are still SolidJS — they're being converted separately. For now, import and use them as-is (they will be fixed in a separate step). You can keep the imports but the JSX usage stays the same since they're function components.
8. `TimeIndicatorModel` is already in `@wc3-ui/react` — remove the import from `./components/TimeIndicatorModel` and use the one from `@wc3-ui/react`.
9. `TopHudWebGL` is already in `@wc3-ui/react` — remove the import from `./components/TopHudWebGL` and use the one from `@wc3-ui/react`.

## File to write

Write the complete converted file to `/home/garrosh/repos/warcraft-components/src/App.tsx`.

Read the source file completely (all 4360 lines) before writing. Read in chunks: lines 1-500, 500-1000, 1000-1500, 1500-2000, 2000-2500, 2500-3000, 3000-3500, 3500-4360. Then write the complete React version.
